2026-03-12T10:22:46.4149787Z Now I have enough information to create the GitHub issue. The attestation tests are running in **live** mode against real Azure infrastructure, and the `#attestTpm` test is failing because the Azure Attestation service instance used in CI does not have a VBS (Virtualization Based Security) attestation policy configured.
2026-03-12T10:22:46.4151167Z 
2026-03-12T10:22:46.4509652Z ● safeoutputs-create_issue
2026-03-12T10:22:46.4510166Z   └ {"result":"success"}
2026-03-12T10:22:46.4510324Z 
2026-03-12T10:22:51.0431950Z A GitHub issue has been created titled **"CI test failures on main: @azure/attestation"** covering the detected CI failure.
2026-03-12T10:22:51.0432924Z 
2026-03-12T10:22:51.0433175Z **Summary of findings:**
2026-03-12T10:22:51.0433462Z 
2026-03-12T10:22:51.0440224Z - **1 package affected**: `@azure/attestation`
2026-03-12T10:22:52.1077101Z - **Failing test**: `[AAD] Attestation Client > #attestTpm` (1/212 tests)
2026-03-12T10:22:53.2239162Z - **Root cause**: The attestation test suite runs in **live mode** against real Azure infrastructure. The Azure Attestation service instance used in CI is returning `"A VBS attestation policy has not been set on the attestation provider."` — a service-side prerequisite that wasn't needed before. The test correctly sets a TPM policy, but the service now also requires a VBS policy to be configured.
2026-03-12T10:22:53.2245991Z - **This is not a code regression** — the HEAD commit was an unrelated AutoPR for `@azure-arm-postgresql-flexible`.
2026-03-12T10:22:54.4139729Z - **Fix options**: Either (1) configure a VBS policy on the CI attestation provider in the Azure portal, or (2) update the test to also set/reset a VBS policy alongside the TPM policy.
2026-03-12T10:22:54.4166245Z - **Not reproducible locally** without live Azure Attestation credentials and TPM hardware.
2026-03-12T10:22:54.4166948Z
